allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking gives theory-of-constraints the whole-structure view it needs to see the real throughput governor instead of local noise.
allies.source_quote: Constraint management is a meta-model that leverages systems thinking—the discipline of seeing interrelationships rather than things.

allies.rationale_text: Bottlenecks is the practical language of theory-of-constraints because the model lives or dies by naming the one limiting lane first.
allies.source_quote: The fundamental essence of this mental model is the recognition that in any complex system, performance is ultimately limited by one key restricting element, or constraint.

allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory reinforces theory-of-constraints by showing why simplification and chunking around the central bottleneck matter.
allies.source_quote: Constraint management supports Cognitive Load Theory by insisting on simplification and chunking, reducing the mental energy required to solve a problem or learn a new skill.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias corrupts theory-of-constraints by making the team protect its favorite bottleneck story instead of measuring the real cap.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation bias drives people to process information to get a conclusion they want rather than discovering what is objectively true.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Theory-of-constraints conflicts with optimization-theory when local utilization improvements in non-constrained steps create the illusion of progress.
structured_tensions.source_quote: theory-of-constraints vs optimization-theory: theory-of-constraints conflicts with optimization-theory (optimization theory) when local utilization improvements in non-constrained steps create the illusion of progress while leaving the true constraint unaddressed.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Theory-of-constraints conflicts with commitment-bias when the team keeps investing in the first bottleneck story instead of reassessing whether it is still binding.
structured_tensions.source_quote: theory-of-constraints vs commitment-bias: theory-of-constraints conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when the team over-invests in improving the initially identified bottleneck rather than reassessing whether that constraint is still the binding one.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on systems and bottleneck allies plus explicit tensions against local-optimization illusion and stale commitment.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor operations sprawl beyond the source-backed bottleneck, measurement, and anti-local-optimization surface.
open_questions: Commander's-intent is source-real ally, but bottlenecks and cognitive-load-theory were the cleaner bounded anchors into the current graph vocabulary.
